# Test: Corrupted Git History (Edge Case)
# Validates handling of git subtree split failures with unusual git state.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Create app repo with shallow history
mkdir app
cd app
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/corrupt/v1
exists apx.yaml

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Initial schema'
exec git tag proto/corrupt/v1/v1.0.0

# Step 2: Test publish from empty tree (no module content)
mkdir -p internal/apis/proto/empty/v1
exec git add .
exec git commit --allow-empty -m 'Empty commit'

# Try to publish a non-existent module path
! exec apx publish --module-path=internal/apis/proto/nonexistent/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/corrupt-test --dry-run
stderr 'does not exist'

# Step 3: Verify the valid module still works
exec apx publish --module-path=internal/apis/proto/corrupt/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/corrupt-test --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'

# Step 4: Test with detached HEAD
exec git checkout --detach HEAD
exec apx publish --module-path=internal/apis/proto/corrupt/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/corrupt-test --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'

# Return to main branch
exec git checkout -

cd ..

# Success: Corrupted git history handling validated
# - Non-existent module paths produce clear errors
# - Valid modules still work despite unusual git states
# - Detached HEAD doesn't prevent operations
